Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We are given two logs Log A and Log B We need to merge them into a single event log

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: For each event in Log A try to find a matching event in Log B and vice versa based on order id must match The event types names should be semantically similar we may need a mapping The timestamps should be within a tolerance e g 2 seconds

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We note that Log B has richer attributes So when merging we want to keep all attributes from both logs

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: If we merge we create a single event with A primary timestamp we can choose the one from Log A as the primary but note that Log A timestamps might be the time received Alternatively we can use the timestamp from Log B for the event if it is the actual occurrence time But we do not know for sure

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We are told Log A is the primary timeline so we might use Log A timestamp for ordering but we want to include both timestamps in the merged record

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We also note that Log B has events that are not in Log A for example Quality Check and Log A has events that are not in Log B for example Item Delivered

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We need to decide on a mapping for event types Log A Order Received Order Validated Payment Processed Item Shipped Item Delivered Log B OrderReceived OrderValidation PaymentCheck Quality Check Shipping We can map Order Received and OrderReceived same event Order Validated and OrderValidation same Payment Processed and PaymentCheck same Item Shipped and Shipping same Then Quality Check in Log B has no counterpart in Log A and Item Delivered in Log A has no counterpart in Log B

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: We will use a dictionary to map Log A event types to Log B event names and vice versa for matching

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: We will set a tolerance for timestamp matching e g 2 seconds

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We will process each event in Log A and try to find a match in Log B Then we will also include any events from Log B that were not matched For unmatched events we include them as they are

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: We want the final log in chronological order We can use the timestamp from Log A for merged events or from Log B if only in Log B but note that the timestamps in Log A and Log B are very close

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: However note that the timestamps in Log A and Log B are not exactly the same We are told that Log A timestamps are when the event was received while Log B timestamps are from the system that recorded the event We do not know which is more accurate But since we are to produce a single timeline we can use the timestamp from the system that recorded the event Log B for the actual occurrence but then we have to note that for events only in Log A we do not have that Alternatively we can use the timestamp from Log A for ordering because it is the primary timeline but then we are using the received time

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Lets decide We will use the timestamp from Log A for events that are in Log A or merged with Log B for ordering and for events only in Log B we will use Log B timestamp But note that the timestamps are very close so the order should be consistent

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We will create a list of merged events Each event will have timestamp the one we use for ordering which we will take from Log A if available otherwise Log B order id event type the name from Log A if available otherwise from Log B attributes from Log A if any and Log B if any combined We will also note the source of the event if merged then both else only A or only B

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Parse Log A and Log B into lists of dictionaries For each event in Log A try to find a matching event in Log B by order id must match The event name in Log B must map to the event type in Log A using a mapping The absolute difference in timestamps is less than or equal to 2 seconds If found merge the two events After processing all events in Log A collect the remaining events in Log B that were not matched Sort the merged events and the unmatched events by timestamp

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets define the mapping mapping Order Received to OrderReceived Order Validated to OrderValidation Payment Processed to PaymentCheck Item Shipped to Shipping

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: We will convert the timestamps to datetime objects for comparison We will set a tolerance of 2 seconds We will mark events as matched so we do not reuse them The logs are small so we can do a nested loop for matching
